response: The internet’s origins trace back to the 1960s when the US Department of Defense funded networking research, leading to ARPANET’s first message in 1969 and gradual university-linked growth until the 1983 adoption of TCP/IP. In 1989, Tim Berners-Lee invented the World Wide Web at CERN—introducing HTML, URLs, and HTTP—and the 1993 Mosaic browser made it widely accessible. The late-1990s dot-com boom spurred huge investments but culminated in the 2001 crash, with survivors like Amazon and Google emerging as industry giants. From mid-2000s social media platforms to the 2007 iPhone revolution and 2006’s AWS launch, today over 5 billion people rely on the internet for commerce, communication, and entertainment.
